lobby.page_subtitle_v2: Tap an avatar on your half to send a player there.

lobby.helper_desktop: Drag & drop, or click the arrows that appear on a player
lobby.helper_mobile: Tap a player, then send them up or down

poster.page_subtitle: We rolled two teams for you

new_game.replay_tour: Replay the onboarding tour for this step
new_game.red_card_minute_title: What minute was the red card shown?
new_game.yellow_card_minute_title: What minute was the yellow card shown?
new_game.penalty_minute_title: What minute was the penalty missed?
new_game.random_no_teams: Not enough equally rated teams in star range

lobby.tip_1_body: Tap an avatar in the upper, red pitch half to put that player on the home side. Tap again to un-assign.
lobby.tip_2_body: The lower, green half works the same way for the away side. Players already assigned on the opposite side appear dimmed here.
lobby.tip_3_body: Each side has a guest tile for players without an account. The match doesn't count toward their profile, but the final score is still recorded correctly.

poster.tip_1_body: On open a balanced 4–5★ team pair is rolled automatically. If the star ratings don't match you'll see a small "stars uneven" hint underneath.
poster.tip_2_body: "Roll" rolls a new pair in the same range, "Adjust" opens the star-range picker, "Manual" lets you type both team names yourself.
poster.tip_3_body: The big red button starts live tracking — goals, cards and missed penalties are entered right on the pitch from there.

live.tip_1_body: Home up top, Away down below. The header above the pitch tracks the live score, the footer below holds the action buttons. Take a moment to read the line-up — we'll record the first goal next.
live.tip_2_body: For this demo we'll tap the first home player as the scorer. The opposite half of the pitch will turn into the editor in a moment.
live.tip_3_body: This "Open play" pill in the editor is tappable — it opens the dialog to switch between open play, penalty, corner or free-kick. For our demo we leave it on Open play.
live.tip_4_body: A second tap on a teammate from the same side records the assist. Optional on every goal — skip the second tap and the goal is saved without one.
live.tip_5_body: The horizontal scroll-slider in the middle is the match minute. We're setting it to 45 for the demo — handy because we can show stoppage time next.
live.tip_6_body: At 45, 90 or 120 the small slider underneath unlocks — that's stoppage time. We're setting it to +3 here, so minute 45+3.
live.tip_7_body: The green check saves the event. The editor closes and the goal appears just below as a pill on the timeline.
live.tip_8_body: Mis-tapped? Hit the × on the pill, confirm the prompt — the event is cleanly removed and the score recalculated.
live.tip_9_body: Use the footer buttons to arm red-card or missed-penalty mode, then tap the player — the rest works just like a goal. Own goals are recorded with a long-press on the scorer. The 11m button starts a penalty shootout after a draw in extra time. "End match" finishes the game.
live.tip_10_body: The demo data is wiped in a moment — you start with an empty timeline. If you want a quick rematch later, the match summary has a "Start rematch" button that jumps straight back here.

dashboard.tip_1_body: This is your record for the current ISO week: matches, wins, losses and ELO change. Auto-updates after every game.
dashboard.tip_2_body: Your current ELO plus the last 5 results as pills. One glance and you know what shape you're in.
dashboard.tip_3_body: The three most recent matches you played in. Tap a card to open the match report and the detailed stats.
dashboard.tip_4_body: Today's three top-ELO players. Tap a row to open that player's profile. Full standings live under "Leaderboard" in the bottom nav.
dashboard.tip_5_body: The bottom nav takes you to games, leaderboard, history and your profile. Hit "+" to start a new match.

profile.tip_1_body: Avatar, username, archetype, current rank, ELO and lifetime record (wins / draws / losses). The little quote above comes from Marcel — our AI reporter.
profile.tip_2_body: Spider chart showing your strength axes — offense, defense, chemistry and more. Tap an axis to see how it's calculated.
profile.tip_3_body: Last 5 results plus an ELO sparkline. Instant feedback on whether you're trending up or down.
profile.tip_4_body: Favorite (who you beat most), Nemesis (who's tough on you), best partner. Tap a card to open that player's profile.
profile.tip_5_body: Your unlocked badges. There are 15 total — some come from streaks, others from one-off performances.

minute_picker.title: What minute was the goal scored?
minute_picker.error_too_early: No later minute is possible in this phase — switch to extra time

marcel.title: The Chronicler
marcel.bio_1: Marcel has been covering the big games for three decades — from Bundesliga to European nights and World Cups. He has called the goals of four generations of strikers live, and for many his voice has become synonymous with the game itself.
marcel.bio_2: His strength is the story behind the goal. He tells you not only what happened, but what it means — for the player, for the team, for the moment. When a match boils over or falls silent, Marcel is the one who captures the atmosphere. For the office league he delivers reports with the gravitas of classic broadcasts and a dry punchline only experienced voices land.
marcel.quote: “Well now… that strike — a sabre blow in the 84th minute. Remember this one, ladies and gentlemen.”

sophie.title: The Analyst
sophie.bio_1: Sophie comes from the new generation of sports journalism that explains the game with data instead of gut feel. After studying sports science she worked at a football analytics start-up before specialising in data-driven match analysis for the media.
sophie.bio_2: When Sophie commentates a match, you can hear the numbers under the words. xG, pass accuracy, pressing intensity — she translates what analytics show into clear sentences anyone can follow. In tight or tactically interesting games she delivers the calm, precise framing others forget about in the drama. If you want to know why a team won, listen to Sophie.
sophie.quote: “Notable in this game: 64 % possession means less control, not more. The numbers rarely lie.”

frank.title: The Enthusiast
frank.bio_1: Frank is the voice you can't ignore. Raised on late-90s Bundesliga, shaped by epic Champions League nights and an unshakable belief that every game is a drama waiting to be uncovered. He has called everything — from cup finals to amateur derbies — and loves each one with the same intensity.
frank.bio_2: When a goal goes in, Frank explodes. When a comeback is on, you can't hold him down. His speciality is the big moments: hat-tricks, last-minute winners, red cards, knife-edge endings. He gets carried away, sometimes over the top, but always with genuine passion. For the office league he is the energy that spectacular games deserve when the drama gets too big for restraint.
frank.quote: “WHAT a game in the office league! Marco! Marco! In the 84th minute! You have to see this — UNBELIEVABLE!”

player_profile.freshman_body: This player hasn't played enough matches yet. The profile unlocks after 5 games.
player_profile.developing_body: Early trends are showing, but the character isn't final yet. More data sharpens the picture.

axis_info.consistency: How even the performance has been across recent matches.
axis_info.discipline: Fewer red cards → higher score. Inverse percentile.
axis_info.winner: How much your presence lifts your teammates.

player_profile.no_relationship: Nobody yet — at least 3 shared matches required.

elo.title: ELO
elo.body:
ELO is your player strength expressed as a number. Starting value is 1000.

It rises after wins — especially against stronger opponents — and falls after losses, particularly against weaker ones. Beating a much stronger team earns more points than beating a weaker one.

The calculation is done separately per game mode (1v1, 2v2), so easy 1v1 wins don't inflate your 2v2 rating and vice versa.

archetype.title: Archetype
archetype.body:
Your character type is derived automatically from your six strength axes — Finisher, Playmaker, Clutch, Consistency, Discipline and Winner.

Depending on which dominate, you get a matching player style. It becomes reliable after about 5–10 matches. Until then you'll see "Freshman" or "Developing".

pass_network.title: Pass character
pass_network.body:
From the match's passing stats we derive a short pass-style tag — e.g. "Tiki-Taka" for lots of short passes or "Direct play" for long balls.

The tags show how a team built up their game. Source is the pass distribution from the uploaded FC stats. No uploaded stats means no tags.

awards.title: Awards
awards.body:
There are 64 trophies to unlock across 9 categories — wins, streaks, goals, defense, special, duo, rivalry, meta, and hidden trophies. Rarity ranges from bronze through silver and gold to diamond.

Trophies are checked automatically after every match. This block shows your top awards — the full collection with progress lives in the Trophy Room.

relations.title: Relationships
relations.body:
"Favourite opponent" shows the rival you beat most often.
"Nemesis" is the opposite — the one giving you a hard time.
"Top partner" is the teammate you share the best combined record with.

A player only shows up here once you've played at least 3 matches together — otherwise the stat would be too random. Tap a card to open that player's profile.

reporter.title: Reporter persona
reporter.body:
Three AI reporters narrate the match report in distinct styles:

• Klassiker — straightforward, traditional commentary
• Analyst — tactical, data-focused
• Euphoriker — emotional, over-the-top

The system picks which reporter covers a match automatically — based on the drama (close, lopsided, lots of goals, etc.).

feedback.title: Send feedback
feedback.intro: Spotted something? General feedback comes straight to me, bugs and feature requests are filed as GitHub issues.
feedback.placeholder_title_feature: Short title — what would you like to see?
feedback.placeholder_body_general: What's on your mind? Praise, critique, ideas — all welcome.
feedback.placeholder_body_bug: What did you do? What should have happened? What actually happened? Repro steps help the most.
feedback.placeholder_body_feature: What would you like to see? Which problem would it solve? How would you use it?
feedback.route_hint: We attach the path "{route}" and your browser user-agent — it helps with reproducing the issue.
feedback.screenshot_hint: Optional. PNG, JPEG, WebP or HEIC, max 5 MB.
feedback.screenshot_error_type: Please pick an image file (PNG, JPEG, WebP, HEIC).
feedback.screenshot_error_read: Couldn't read the image. Please try again.
feedback.success_general: Thanks! Your feedback is on its way.
feedback.success_bug: Thanks! Your bug report was filed as a GitHub issue.
feedback.success_feature: Thanks! Your idea was filed as a GitHub issue.
feedback.settings_section_body: Got an idea, spotted a bug, or just want to say hi? Let us know.
